Election.title: BV2210 — Food-Truck Row — two spots, five counts: vote-splitting, sweeps, and shares
Election.description: From the STAR education repo (github.com/masiarek/star-voting-library, method_comparisons/food_truck_row/) — the vote-splitting showcase. One 100-voter electorate elects TWO food-truck spots five ways. The savory side is a 57-voter outright majority split across three trucks (Arepa 20 first choices, Bao 19, Churro 18); the sweet side is a disciplined 43-voter minority on two (Donut 22, Eclair 21). SNTV (choose one, top-2): Donut + Eclair — the 57% majority gets ZERO seats because it split its one vote three ways; this is the classic SNTV failure that punishes running too many candidates. Bloc STAR and Bloc Ranked Robin (2 seats each): Arepa + Bao — the same majority now SWEEPS both seats; majoritarian multi-winner counts hand 100% of the seats to 57% of the room. STAR-PR / Allocated Score and STV (2 seats each): Arepa + Donut — one seat per side, proportional to the room. Same opinions on every ballot; only the counting rule changes. Savory ballots score/rank only savory trucks (5/4/3 by taste), sweet ballots only sweet (5/4) — cross-side abstentions are Equal Support. LH-verified on all five counts, every rung distinct (22/21/20/19/18), no tie-breaks anywhere.

logs: Donut has the most votes and wins the round
Eclair has the most votes and wins the round
Arepa wins round with highest number of wins.
Bao wins round with highest number of wins.
2 winners will represent 100 voters, so winners will need to represent a quota of 50 voters each.
Arepa is scored highest with 248 stars and is elected!
The 20 voters who gave Arepa more than 4 stars are fully represented and will be removed from future rounds.
The 37 voters who gave Arepa 4 stars are partially represented. 81% of their remaining vote will go toward Arepa and 19% will be preserved for future rounds.
Donut is scored highest with 194 stars and is elected!
The 43 voters who gave Donut more than 0 stars are fully represented and will be removed from future rounds.
The 7 voters who gave Donut 0 stars are partially represented. 100% of their remaining vote will go toward Donut and 0% will be preserved for future rounds.
